prevalence.value: Common in adolescents and young adults; most adults seropositive

incidence.source: Primary care and school health cohort data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; short-term fatigue and school/work absence

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical syndrome with lymphadenopathy and fatigue; heterophile antibody test or EBV serology

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care and activity restriction

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; serology in reference labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care, hydration, avoid contact sports if splenomegaly; corticosteroids only for complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to confirmatory testing in primary care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear clinical management and low-cost supportive care
